county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1836 from Ray. County seat: Gallatin.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Gallatin holds land records from 1836. Daviess County was a center of the 1838 Missouri-Mormon War — the Haun's Mill Massacre occurred in neighboring Caldwell County but involved Daviess County residents. Battle of Crooked River (1838) took place here. Check LDS Church History Library for Mormon-related records. Daviess County Historical Society has genealogical files. Strong Southern settlement from Kentucky and Virginia. Grand River valley agricultural community. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1836

source_profiles.source_name: Daviess County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, Gallatin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1836)

source_profiles.source_name: Daviess County Probate Records -- Probate Court, Gallatin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1836. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Daviess County Marriage Records -- Recorder of Deeds / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1836. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Daviess County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1836-1950 for Daviess. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Daviess County. Missouri is a public land state — BLM GLO records document first transfer of land from federal government. Some areas have earlier French/Spanish colonial grants.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists and personal property assessments from 1836. List heads of household, property, livestock, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Useful as census substitutes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Generally well preserved at Missouri State Archives

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Clinton. County seat: Maysville.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Maysville holds land records from 1845. DeKalb County Historical Society has genealogical files. The county was carved from Clinton County — check Clinton for pre-1845 records. Northwest Missouri agricultural community. Some Civil War guerrilla activity but courthouse survived. Strong Midwestern farming heritage. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. BLM GLO records document original land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1845

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1851 from Crawford, Shannon. County seat: Salem. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Salem holds land records from 1851. Dent County is in the Ozark Highlands with significant iron mining heritage — the Dent County seat of Salem was near the Meramec Iron Works. Dent County Historical Society has family files. Strong Scots-Irish and Southern Appalachian settlement. Montauk State Park and the headwaters of the Current River attract researchers interested in pioneer-era settlement. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Check Crawford and Shannon counties for pre-1851 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1851

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1857 from Ozark. County seat: Ava. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Ava holds records restarting after the 1886 courthouse fire. Earlier records were also damaged by Civil War guerrilla actions. For pre-1886 research, check Ozark County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO records. Douglas County is a remote Ozark county with very sparse population. Strong Appalachian settlement heritage with subsistence farming. Douglas County Historical Society has some post-fire family files. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire records. Church records (Baptist, Methodist) are essential supplements in this county.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1857

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Stoddard. County seat: Kennett. Missouri Bootheel (southeastern lowlands) — cotton agriculture region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Kennett holds land records from 1845. Dunklin County is in the Missouri Bootheel (southeastern lowlands), a major cotton-growing region. Dunklin County Historical Society and Museum in Kennett has genealogical collections. Significant African American population — check Freedmen's Bureau records at NARA. Sharecropper and tenant farmer records are important for this cotton region. Strong Southern settlement from Tennessee, Mississippi, and Arkansas. The 1939 Missouri Sharecroppers' Strike occurred here. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Swampland drainage district records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1845

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1818 from St. Louis. County seat: Union. Missouri German Belt — significant German immigrant settlement from the 1830s-1860s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Union holds land records from 1818. Franklin County has exceptional German immigrant heritage — Washington and other towns were heavily German-settled from the 1830s-1860s. German-language church records (Lutheran, Catholic, Reformed) are essential. Franklin County Historical Society in Washington has genealogical files. Meramec River valley settlement records. Missouri River commerce shaped the county's economy. Daniel Boone lived in this area (Defiance area, St. Charles/Franklin County line). FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. German Heritage Museum in Hermann (adjacent Gasconade County) also has relevant materials.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1818

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1820 from Franklin. County seat: Hermann. Missouri German Belt — significant German immigrant settlement from the 1830s-1860s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Hermann holds land records from 1820. Gasconade County is the heart of Missouri's German settlement — Hermann was founded in 1837 as a German colony by the German Settlement Society of Philadelphia. Deutschheim State Historic Site preserves German heritage. Hermann has exceptional church records: Evangelical, Lutheran, and Catholic congregations with records from the 1830s. Gasconade County Historical Society has genealogical files. Weinstrasse (wine route) history — early viticulture records. Stone Hill Winery records. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive. German-language newspapers published in Hermann are at SHSMO in Columbia.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1820

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Clinton. County seat: Albany.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Albany holds land records from 1841. Gentry County Historical Society in Albany has genealogical files. The county was named for Colonel Richard Gentry, killed in the Second Seminole War. Northwest Missouri agricultural community with Midwestern settlement patterns. Some Civil War guerrilla activity but courthouse survived. Grand River tributaries were early settlement corridors. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1833 from Crawford, Wayne. County seat: Springfield.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Springfield holds land records from 1833. Greene County is the urban center of southwest Missouri. Springfield-Greene County Library has an outstanding Local History and Genealogy Department with microfilm, family files, and indexes. History Museum on the Square has Civil War and regional collections. Wilson's Creek National Battlefield (1861) is nearby — the first major Civil War battle west of the Mississippi. Drury University and Missouri State University have local history archives. Springfield had significant German and Irish immigrant communities. Route 66 heritage. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive for Greene County.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1833

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Livingston. County seat: Trenton.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Trenton holds land records from 1841. Grundy County Historical Society in Trenton has genealogical files. North-central Missouri prairie county. Thompson River valley settlement. Strong Midwestern farming community. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. BLM GLO records document original land entries. Trenton served as a railroad hub — check railroad employment records for the Rock Island and Burlington lines.
